changelog:
Model list dedup + targeted performance pass.

/v1/models cleanup:
- Output now emits only the dashed Anthropic id and the raw dotted Kiro id per model. Dropped the dated forms (e.g. -20251101) that some clients were re-resolving as separate routes, and dropped the synthetic -thinking suffix variants — extended thinking is a request-level toggle, not a separate model. Verified the suffix never reached the upstream Kiro API.
- buildAnthropicModelsResponse dedupes output via a seen-id map; alias appender (auto, gpt-4o, gpt-4) now skips ids the cached list already produced, so Kiro's auto model no longer double-registers as a picker entry.
- Tests updated: TestBuildAnthropicModelsResponseHandlesAutoAndKnownAliases pins the alias-dedup behavior.
- Docs and i18n examples switched from claude-sonnet-4-5-20251101 to claude-sonnet-4-5.

Performance pass (10 findings, all green on go test ./...):
- proxy/kiro.go parseEventStream: 12-byte prelude moved to stack ([12]byte vs make), per-frame message buffer comes from sync.Pool (storing *[]byte to avoid the slice-pinning footgun). Allocates only when a frame exceeds the pooled capacity.
- proxy/translator.go: filter rules compile their regexp once and memoize via sync.Map (typed-nil sentinel for failed compiles). Image placeholder regex and data-URL regex hoisted to package level. applySystemPromptFilters / applyUserMessageFilters snapshot the prompt-filter config once instead of re-fetching it for every rule.
- proxy/handler.go: thinkingCfg hoisted once near the top of handleClaudeMessagesInternal and the OpenAI handler instead of being re-read at every reference site.
- pool/account.go: read-mostly hot paths (GetModelList, GetByID, Count, AvailableCount, GetAllAccounts, CooldownRemaining) now use sync.RWMutex.RLock; write paths (GetNextForModel which mutates SWR + decay; RecordError/Success, UpdateStats, UpdateToken, ClearSoftCooldownIfHealthy, SetModelList, Reload) keep exclusive Lock.
- config/config.go + main.go: per-request stats fsync coalesced. UpdateAccountStats sets an atomic dirty flag instead of saving immediately. StartStatsSaver runs a 5s ticker that drains via FlushStats (atomic Swap + saveLocked, restores dirty on error). Shutdown calls FlushStats() under statsFlushMu so it can't return mid-write.
- proxy/handler.go refreshAllAccounts and refreshModelsCache now fan out per-account work via sync.WaitGroup + size-8 semaphore (refreshFanoutConcurrency). refreshModelsCache writes per-worker results into an indexed slice and merges sequentially after wg.Wait, so mergeUniqueModels stays single-threaded. All workers wrap their bodies in a recover so a single upstream surprise can't tear down the proxy.
- /v1/models cold-start no longer blocks on the per-account fan-out. handleModels now triggers triggerModelsRefreshAsync (single-flight via atomic.Bool CAS gate) and serves fallbackAnthropicModels immediately; the gate is cleared in defer + recover so a panic in the async goroutine is logged, not propagated.
- SSE writer fast-path: sendSSE, sendResponsesEvent (Codex Responses), and the three OpenAI streaming sites now build the frame in a single pre-sized byte slice and emit it with one w.Write. Pre-built openAIDoneFrame for the [DONE] terminator. Avoids fmt.Fprintf format-string parsing on every chunk.
